description: In shell, validate external input at the argument parsing, IFS handling, and piping/redirection of untrusted input before internal use.
body: In shell, parsing, environment, CLI, and network inputs reach the program through argument parsing, IFS handling, and piping/redirection of untrusted input and must be checked before they are trusted. Use when handling untrusted or external input crossing into shell code. Do not pass raw external values into internal logic, and avoid assuming well-formed input. Actions: validate at the boundary, reject or normalize malformed values, verify the rejection path with a test, and record the accepted contract. Validation signals include tests on malformed input, a logged/trace rejection, and a defined schema for the boundary. Risk boundary: escalate when input feeds security-sensitive or non-reversible operations; deny by default rather than guessing intent.
